programmes.show_description: Empty Nest: NCIS searches for a missing undercover agent in the midst of an active hostage situation.
Ruth's love for baby Fia is undeniable. But under the strain of loneliness and a difficult domestic existence, the mother impulsively makes a drastic decision.
Maia: Bruised by his encounter with Nicola, Danny goes to The Crimson Orchid gentlemen's club to look for Maia, who Nicola has accused of corrupting Christina.
Jaisal: Against Maia's advice, Danny goes alone to meet with Christina's supplier, who is revealed to be Jaisal Batra, a corrupt former police officer turned club owner.
Programmes start at 19:00.
TK severs ties with Chris. Madonna lets a friend go. Viv hires a dud.
Lacey is starting to feel like her old safe again following the positive AVO outcome, but Cash reminds her to remain vigilant.
Who Dares Dare Hudaro: After falling under the spell of a visiting hypnotist, Mark's grades begin to suffer.
Kent: In Kent, the 'garden of England', Rick Stein harvests English cherries at a family-run farm.
Glasgow: Rick Stein delves into Glasgow's multicultural food scene, tucking into Korean and Pakistani dishes and finding out how Italian food became part of the city's fabric.
Spain's Big Bang: Eva Longoria explores Spain's rapid culinary evolution from basic food traditions to worldwide gastronomic influence within a single generation.
Caught in limbo for years in Ireland's immigration system, a young Nigerian woman develops a friendship with an employee who she meets at one of the accommodation centres.
Coverage of the Foo Fighters as they bring their stadium anthems to St James' Church, An Daingean.
Fergus O'Farrell was the charismatic voice of Interference, one of the most compelling and influential bands to emerge from the Irish music scene in the 1990s.
Union Blues: Bumpy battles a union strike threatening club construction.
The latest international news headlines.
The latest International and European news.
When Flix and friends stumble onto a Mac Cat action movie set, their chaotic ski attempts turn heroic as they must rescue rival Burma from a runaway snowball disaster.
Flix hosts a turbo-charged bingo night to earn his final scout badge, but his wild new approach causes chaos and sends him scrambling to save the night.
Short stories based on well-loved fairy tales and nursery rhymes.
Boggy forces Duncan to come clean as Phoenix play well and make it through to the semi-finals. But when the referee makes a bad decision Jamie is left with a huge moral dilemma.
Can Jamie lead Phoenix FC to a cup final? Or will Duncan's secret come back to haunt him?
Reality-style drama with Victoria Baldesarra and Alexandra Beaton, following the lives of a group of young dancers as they attempt to win a series of regional dance championships.

In the morning, David and Lacey have a cup of coffee, and Lacey asks if the AVO has gone out to Holden.
On his way up from a surf, Justin passes John, who's rushing to bring in the flags.
Eden stays by Remi's side following a session of radiation therapy, and it's hard for her to watch him struggle with the uncomfortable side effects.
Sparks fly between a city florist and the owner of an apiary business after she inherits her great aunt's farmland.
England v Ireland (Kick-off 2.25pm). All the action from both teams' opening match of the championship, held at Allianz Stadium Twickenham.
The latest news and updates from the UEFA Champions League.
Peacocks and Pumas: Frank and Victoria go on a stake out in the midst of a prank war, while Patel and Emily care for a recently concussed Shred.

programmes.show_name: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond The Lobby
programmes.show_description: Rob Rinder and Monica Galetti visit Wilderness Hotel Inari, an adventure hotel above the Arctic Circle in Lapland.
In Thurso, Scotland's most northerly mainland station, the crew race to the rescue of an ocean-going rower who's run into trouble 40 miles out to sea
The Route to the Top: Documentary following three lionesses and their nine cubs in Kenya's Masai Mara reserve, with cameras focusing on the youngsters' journey to adulthood over five years.
The 1976 Formula One Championship season is dominated by a fierce rivalry between two contrasting racers, James Hunt and Niki Lauda.
